title: Warm Crunchy Irish Seafood Stew
description: A resinous, nut-free, terracotta-baked Irish seafood stew with a crispy breadcrumb topping that showcases bold maritime flavors.
instructions: Heat the terracotta pot over medium heat and add the olive oil, then sauté the mirepoix until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Sprinkle the flour and smoked paprika over the vegetables, stirring constantly for 1 minute to eliminate raw flour taste.
Pour in the cognac, swirling to deglaze the pot; let the alcohol evaporate for 1 minute.
Add the seafood stock and dried thyme, then bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Gently fold in the mixed seafood, cover, and simmer for 5 minutes until the mussels have opened.
Remove the pot from heat, then stir in the lemon juice and chopped parsley.
Place the pot in a preheated oven at 350°F, baking for 5 minutes to crisp the panko breadcrumbs on top.
Let the stew rest for 2 minutes before serving, allowing the flavors to meld.
